reasoning: The recall was issued on March 28, 2025, for ~212,000 lbs of liquid egg products distributed nationwide. The deadline for the recall to resolve as Closed is July 1, 2025, which provides a window of approximately 95 days (around 3 months). Closing an FSIS recall requires the recalling firm to complete its retrieval efforts and for FSIS staff to conduct Effectiveness Checks to ensure consignees were notified and took appropriate action. Doing this for a nationwide, 200,000+ lb recall usually takes a significant amount of time — often well over 3 months, given the federal bureaucracy and the number of distribution channels involved. While some smaller, localized recalls can close quickly, this is a relatively large one. Therefore, the likelihood of it formally changing from "Active" to "Closed" on the FSIS website before July 1, 2025, is estimated to be low, around 25%.
